zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanna de Burgh於1190年出生在愛爾蘭時，她的父親William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster30歲，母親Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain17歲。她於1219年與David De La Roche在英國威爾斯布洛可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1240年在愛爾蘭康瑙特享年50歲，埋葬在威爾士。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joanna de Burgh于1190年出生在爱尔兰时，她的父亲William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster30岁，母亲Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain17岁。她于1219年与David De La Roche在英国威尔斯布洛可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1240年在爱尔兰康瑙特享年50岁，埋葬在威尔士。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joanna de Burgh was born in 1190, in Ireland, her father, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, was 30 and her mother, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, was 17. She married David De La Roche in 1219, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1240, in Connaught, Ireland, at the age of 50, and was buried in Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joanna de Burgh est née en 1190, à Irlande, son père, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé David De La Roche en 1219, à Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1240, à Connaught, Irlande, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joanna de Burgh 1190 in Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster 30 und ihre Mutter Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain 17. Sie hat 1219 in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom David De La Roche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1240 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Connaught, Irland und wurde in Wales begraben.

ht.Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna de Burgh è nata il nel 1190 nel Irlanda, sua padre, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato David De La Roche nel 1219, nel Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1240, nel Connaught, Irlanda, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Joanna de Burghが1190年にアイルランドで生まれた時，父William de Burgh Seneschal of Munsterは30歳，母Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briainは17歳であった。1219年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアでDavid De La Rocheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1240年にアイルランドコナウトで50歳で死去し，ウェールズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna de Burgh(이)가 1190년 아일랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1219년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 David De La Roche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1240년 아일랜드 콘오트에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스에 묻혔다.

mg.Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna de Burgh nasceu em 1190, em Irlanda, seu pai, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com David De La Roche em 1219, em Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1240, em Connaught, Irlanda, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joanna de Burgh родилась в 1190 г. в местности Ирландия, её отцу по имени William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David De La Roche в 1219 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1240 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joanna de Burgh nació en 1190, en Irlanda, su papá, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, tenía 17 años. Se casó con David De La Roche en 1219, en Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1240, en Connaught, Irlanda, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Gales.
